name: Arjun Das
address: Salt Lake Sector V, Kolkata, West Bengal - 700091
summary: Offensive Security professional with 2 years of experience in penetration testing, red teaming, and vulnerability research. Holds OSCP certification — one of the most respected credentials in offensive security. Discovered 6 CVEs across open-source projects. Bug bounty hunter with ₹4.2 Lakhs in total payouts from HackerOne and Bugcrowd. Adept at web, network, and Active Directory exploitation.

experience.title: Junior Penetration Tester
responsibilities: Delivered 20+ VAPT engagements including web apps (OWASP), internal networks, and AD environments for BFSI and e-commerce clients.
Discovered critical SQL Injection vulnerability in a PSU bank's loan management portal — CVSS 9.1 — responsibly disclosed and patched.
Developed a custom C2 framework extension for an adversary simulation exercise, evading 3 major AV/EDR solutions.
Wrote detailed pentest reports with executive summaries for C-suite clients; presentations to CISOs.
Built automated Active Directory enumeration and privilege escalation toolkit in Python used by team of 12 testers.
Conducted 3 phishing simulation campaigns with 2,000+ target employees; achieved 28% click rate on baseline.

experience.title: Bug Bounty Hunter
responsibilities: Reported 45+ valid vulnerabilities across 20+ programs on HackerOne and Bugcrowd.
Total earnings: ₹4,20,000 in bounty payouts (P1: 6, P2: 14, P3+: 25).
Specializations: IDOR, SSRF, Race Conditions, JWT flaws, SAML Injection.

projects.name: ADExploit – AD Enumeration & Attack Toolkit
projects.description: Automated Active Directory enumeration toolkit. Performs ACL abuse detection, Kerberoasting, and ASREProasting automation. Used in 5+ real engagements. 500+ GitHub stars.

projects.name: WebFuzz – Smart Web Application Fuzzer
projects.description: High-speed, context-aware web fuzzer with support for HTTP/2, custom auth headers, and mutation strategies. 3x faster than ffuf on tested benchmarks.

achievements: OSCP Certification — Passed on first attempt (2023)
HackerOne Hall of Fame — Paytm, OLA, MakeMyTrip programs
CVE Author: CVE-2023-31456, CVE-2023-35821, CVE-2024-11029 (among others)
Speaker: null0x0 Security Conference Kolkata 2023 — 'Breaking JWT: Real-World Case Studies'
